faq.q: How do I calculate the distance between two points?
faq.a: Use the Euclidean distance formula: d = √((x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)²). Subtract the first point's coordinates from the second, square the differences, sum them, and take the square root.

faq.q: What is the Euclidean distance formula?
faq.a: d = √((x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)²), where (x1,y1) and (x2,y2) are the coordinates of the two points on a 2D plane.
Yes, use the 3D distance formula: d = √((x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)² + (z2-z1)²).

faq.q: How do I find the midpoint between two points?
How do I find the slope between two points?
faq.a: Use the slope formula: m = (y2-y1)/(x2-x1). This measures the steepness of the line connecting the two points.

faq.q: How do I find the angle between two points?
faq.a: Use the arctangent formula: θ = arctan((y2-y1)/(x2-x1)). This gives you the angle in radians.

faq.q: What are real-world applications of distance calculations?
faq.a: GPS navigation, analytical geometry, physics simulations, GIS mapping, robotics, and engineering projects all rely on distance calculations.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are free to use with no registration required.
